evidence.title: Council on Foreign Relations explainer on Operation Southern Spear and Venezuela
evidence.source_description: Council on Foreign Relations explainer, captured as relevant text extract during plaintiff merits investigation.
evidence.relevance: Credible secondary analysis describing Operation Southern Spear, the military buildup, blockade context, counternarcotics framing, and concern that the campaign went beyond counternarcotics toward regime change/control questions.

evidence.title: CSIS imagery analysis: surgical strike, not shock and awe
evidence.source_description: Center for Strategic and International Studies commentary by Ryan C. Berg, Mark F. Cancian, Joseph S. Bermudez Jr., Jennifer Jun, Henry Ziemer, and Chris H. Park, published January 9, 2026; captured as relevant text extract during defendant merits investigation.
evidence.relevance: Independent imagery-based analysis limiting the scope and objective of the January 3, 2026 operation; relevant to whether the operation was intended to establish territorial control or was narrowly focused on capture/extraction with limited target sets.

evidence.title: PBS/AP timeline of U.S. escalation leading to Maduro capture
evidence.source_description: PBS NewsHour page carrying Associated Press timeline by Regina Garcia Cano and AP contributors, captured as relevant text extract during plaintiff merits investigation.
evidence.relevance: Independent timeline corroborating December 2025 blockade/covert strike context and January 3, 2026 large-scale U.S. strike across Caracas, capture of Maduro and Flores, and removal from Venezuela.

evidence.title: Official U.S. Department of War account of Operation Absolute Resolve in Caracas
evidence.source_description: U.S. Department of War / Pentagon News article by Matthew Olay, published January 3, 2026, captured as relevant text extract during AAR plaintiff merits investigation.
evidence.relevance: Official U.S. source describing the January 3, 2026 military operation in Caracas, Venezuela, including location, military character, planning, special operations forces, ground extraction force, and aircraft support.
